item_description_text: A car is like a small moving room with seats. It has four wheels and can take you from one place to another. It needs petrol to move, just like we need food to have energy.

Pikachu are a type of Pokémon created in 1996 by Atsuko Nishida. They are short, chubby, cute, yellow rodent creatures with a lightning bolt-shaped tail, long pointed ears, and red circles on their cheeks. It is an "Electric-type", which means that it has electric powers. Pikachu is the mascot of Pokémon, which means that it is the most recognizable Pokémon in the world!

item_description_text: From the Earth, a star looks like a small glowing dot in the sky. It is actually a huge ball of gas with heat and light in outer space, far far away from Earth. They have existed for billions of years. Our sun is a type of star, except it is a lot closer to us so we can feel its heat and light.

When your favorite singer wants to put their song on the radio, or put it out into the world, they need to record it! Now songs usually have many different instruments, like guitars, pianos , drums and singing! All of these instruments come together to make the beautiful music you listen to, but how? Aha! That is where the sound board helps. All of the musicians play their instruments, and artist can use a sound board to put all of the instruments together for you to listen to!

An astronaut is a person who travels off of planet Earth into space. An astronaut travels to space in a rocket. An astronaut is trained to work and live in space in a rocket or a space shuttle. An astronaut can stay in space for months to learn about space and conduct research. The word "astronaut" comes from Greek words meaning "star sailor." An astronaut eats special food in space that is dry and easy to store.
next_12: Space suit, Moon, Telescope, Satellite, Space station, Countdown, Spaceship window, Alien (imaginative association)
tags: space, space ship, Buzz Aldrin, space travel, NASA

item_description_text: A little machine that sits on your wrist and tells you the time

A microphone is a special device used to record any sound! The earliest microphones were invented by Thomas Edison, and they converted sound into drawings on a wax cylinder. These days, they can turn sound into electricity, data, or many other things!

The white-breasted nuthatch is a bird species in North America. Its special power is that it can walk all the way around a tree trunk, even going upside down, up the trunk or down the trunk, to find its favorite food.
image_credit: I believe these are open sources photos from the Cornell lab of ornithology

item_description_text:
A pen is a small tool you hold in your hand to make marks on paper. When you move it, it leaves a line, so you can draw pictures, write your name, or make little doodles. It usually has a skinny body, a pointy tip, and ink inside that comes out when you touch it to paper.

item_description_text: Boba Tea is a fun tea drink from Taiwan with little bubbles called boba inside. It comes with an extra big straw so you can suck up all the little boba pearls. The best part about boba tea is that you can make it just the way you like! You can use any flavors of milk teas, fruit teas, and even slushies! And there are tons of different kinds of boba too, like chewy boba made with honey, or fruity boba that pops when you bite it.
next_12: Milk, Tea, Honey, Taiwan, Tapioca, Grass Jelly, Popping Boba

item_description_text: A 3D printer is a device that can make objects in three dimensions. You can use your computer to draw something and then the 3D printer makes it come to life. Maybe you have a toy that you want to design that only exists in your mind. If you can draw it on a computer, maybe with the help of your mom or dad, then the 3D printer can turn it into a real object. Usually 3D printed things can't be too big. Usually smaller than your hand but sometimes they can be really big.

A bindi is a special little dot or sticker that people, especially in India, wear on their forehead, right between their eyebrows. They can come in many colors and shapes. A common form is red or black and circular. Bindis can mean many different things; they can be a sign of marriage, a religious symbol, or a fashion statement. People wear them to look pretty, celebrate special days, or just to show they are proud of their culture. (Source: Britannica)
date_of_origin_calendar: No clear date, but maybe dates back to Indus River Valley civilization (around 3300 BCE)
next_12: India, Diwali, Holi, Bollywood, Bharatnatyam, Hinduism
tags: Red dot, India, Indian, Indian woman, Diwali, Holi

item_description_text: Hot chocolate is a warm, yummy drink made from chocolate melted into milk! It is sweet and cozy. This drink is perfect for chilly days. People have been making drinks from cocoa for a very long time. Long ago, people in Mesoamerica, more specifically Mexico, discovered that the seeds inside the cacao tree's fruit could be made into a special drink. Back then, it was more bitter and spicy, different than the sweet drink we are used to today! When explorers were introduced to the drink from Mexico, they brought it to Europe and people started adding sugar and warm milk! That's how we got the hot chocolate we know and love today.
next_12: cacao tree, cacao bean, milk, sugar, marshmallow, whipped cream, chocolate, mug, Mesoamerica, Mexico, cacao pod, Spain
tags: cocoa, cacao, chocolate, warm drink, hot cocoa, beverage, milk, sugar, marshmallow, whipped cream
sources:

item_description_text: The common loon is a type of bird in North America and Europe. They spend a lot of time in the water like freshwater Lakes, and are excellent divers. They love to dive and search for fish, crabs, and mollusks to eat. They come from one of the oldest families of birds, dating back 70 million years. In Native American folklore, especially for the Ojibwe people in North America, the Loon is very important. According to their creation stories, the loon helped create the earth and the world we call home. They're known for their beautiful red eyes, and white and black feathers.
location_name: North America and coast of northern Europe and greenland
sources:
Wikipedia: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Common_loon

A groundhog is a chubby, furry animal with short legs and strong claws for digging. It is also called a woodchuck. Groundhogs make homes under the ground in long tunnels, and they like to eat plants and grasses.

item_description_text: An email is a message that is delivered to your computer, phone, or tablet electronically to your specific email account. It was invented in 1971 by Ray Tomlinson in Cambridge, Massachusetts. He was the first person to use an @ symbol to identify individual accounts.

Oboes are woodwind instruments that originated France around the mid 1600s. Sound is produced by blowing air through their double reed while pressing down keys to change the sound. It looks similar to a clarinet but a little bit smaller.
next_12: reed, music, orchestra, woodwind, music, France, musician, clarinet, flute, band, double reed, instrument

item_description_text: The moon is a huge ball of rock in space that is billions of years old. You would usually only see it during the nighttime. Sometimes, you are only seeing part of it as light shines on it differently.

A pie is a baked treat in the shape of a circle. Pies usually have a flaky outside made of dough, which is called a crust. On the inside, there is a filling. The inside of the pie can be sweet, and filled with things like fruits, sweet potatoes, or nuts. The inside can also be salty, and can be filled with things like vegetables, cheese, or meat. The first pies were made in Egypt 8,000 years ago, and were filled with sweet honey. The Egyptians would bake their pies over a fire. Today, people usually cook pies in an oven.
next_12: dough, crust, filling, fruits, pastry, nuts, honey, meat, vegetables, sweet potatoes, dessert, baking
tags: pie, pastry, dessert, thanksgiving, baking, cooking,
sources:

item_description_text: It is a light device which is attached to infront of the vehicle which illuminate the road at night.

A rainbow is a big, colorful curve in the sky that you might see after it rains. It shows up when sunlight shines through tiny drops of water in the air, which spread the light into many colors like red, orange, yellow, green, blue, and purple. People all around the world have been seeing rainbows for thousands of years, and many stories say they are magical bridges or signs of good luck!
date_of_origin_calendar: Prehistoric (no exact date; rainbows have existed as long as sunlight and water have been on Earth, and humans have observed them for thousands of years)
